commit_message:
Stabilize big-string lookup heap attack test on CI (#145845)

Lower the request breaker for testLookupExplosionBigStringManyMatches so
the circuit trips on the first attempt. On large heaps streaming lookup
can otherwise succeed and force several scaled reindex attempts, which
times out single-processor CI (elastic/elasticsearch#145710).

commit_message:
Fix ScaledFloatMatcher to handle coerced numeric strings (#145757)

Stored source preserves original string values (e.g. "28") while
synthetic source reconstructs from doc values as a double (28.0).
The matcher now parses coercible strings as numbers so they match
their numeric equivalents.

issue_title: Examine vector scorers that use withSlice(input, input.length(), ...)
issue_body:
Recent refactoring to use the `withSlice` in vector scorers has introduced an anti-pattern - `withSlice(input, input.length(), ...)`. This pattern could lead to excessive heap allocations. 

When the zero-copy mmap path is unavailable, this could result in catastrophic heap copies of the full file for every bulk scoring call. 

The following scorers currently using the anti-pattern are:
1. `NativeBinaryQuantizedVectorScorer`
2. `Int4VectorScorer`
commit_message:
Fix BBQ and INT4 bulk scorers to use sparse vector resolution (#145779)

This PR fixes a performance problem in the BBQ and INT4 vector scorers where the scoring operations could cause excessive heap allocations. The root cause was that both NativeBinaryQuantizedVectorScorer and Int4VectorScorer called withSlice(input, input.length(), ...), which mapped the entire vector data file into memory. When the zero-copy mmap path was unavailable, this resulted in catastrophic heap copies of the full file for every bulk scoring call.

The scorers are rewritten to use this new sparse path. NativeBinaryQuantizedVectorScorer.scoreBulk now computes per-vector byte offsets and passes them to IndexInputUtils.withSliceAddresses, which resolves only the bytes needed for each vector. Int4VectorScorer receives a similar change.

Finally, the ES93BinaryQuantizedVectorScorerTests.testBulkScore previously picked random node IDs that could contain duplicates. It now shuffles a permutation of [0, numVectors) via Collections.shuffle, guaranteeing unique IDs while keeping the order randomized. This is important since the address resolution asserts that dups are not allowed, since they're likely an error in real workloads.

closes #145797

commit_message:
[Test] Fix CcrSeqNoPruningIT (#146340)

The CcrSeqNoPruningIT tests failed because seq_no doc values were not
fully pruned during force merge.

PruningMergePolicy uses SoftDeletesPolicy.getMinRetainedSeqNo() to know
which operations must be retained in the Lucene index and which
operations can be pruned. This min retained seq no is computed from the
persisted global checkpoint in the translog
(translog.getLastSyncedGlobalCheckpoint()) and the local checkpoint
of the safe commit in Lucene. Both of those values should be up to date
at the time of the force merge in the test to allow seq no to be fully
pruned.

This change fixes the test by forcing the global checkpoint to be sync
in the translog and also force a flush to have an up to date local
checkpoint in the last commit.

Closes #145877

commit_message:
[ML] Fix scroll context leak on remote cluster after network disruption in CCS datafeeds (#146530)

When a datafeed uses Cross-Cluster Search (CCS) and the remote cluster becomes temporarily unavailable, scroll contexts opened on the remote cluster cannot be cleared immediately. These scroll IDs are stored in ScrollDataExtractorFactory.orphanedScrollIds for later retry. The retry is triggered at the start of each new scroll via initScroll(), but if the datafeed processes all remaining data in a single chunk after network recovery and the retry in initScroll() happens before the remote connection is fully re-established, the orphaned IDs remain in the factory indefinitely. With no further initScroll() calls scheduled, those scroll contexts stay open on the remote cluster until their 30-minute TTL expires.

The fix adds a retryClearOrphanedScrollIds() call at the end of ScrollDataExtractor.destroy(), giving a final cleanup opportunity whenever an extractor is torn down — most importantly during datafeed shutdown, by which point the network will typically have recovered. This complements the existing retry in initScroll() and closes the gap where the last extractor in a run could be destroyed without ever attempting to clear the factory's accumulated orphaned IDs. The integration test DatafeedCcsIT.testDatafeedWithCcsRemoteUnavailable (previously muted due to flakiness) now passes consistently across 50+ iterations.

Closes #145948

[9.4] Bump CCS YAML suite timeouts on encryption-at-rest (#147719) (#152950)

Common suites are again hitting their @TimeoutSuite budgets on the
encryption-at-rest periodic step. Bump CcsCommon 20->25 min and
RcsCcsCommon 25->30 min, mirroring the previous +5 minute bump.

RcsCcsSearchYamlTestSuiteIT inherits its budget from the RCS Common
parent, so this also restores symmetry with CssSearchYamlTestSuiteIT
(explicit 30 min), addressing #146756 and #145956 in passing.

Closes #146755, #146756, #145956
Relates to #133409

commit_message:
[ESQL]: unmapped_fields="load" results in 500 EsqlIllegalArgumentException (#146959)

- When `unmapped_fields=load`, skip `MultiTypeEsField` resolution for partially unmapped field keywords if used in a convert function that doesn’t take `KEYWORD`s. This allows `Verifier` to throw `400/VerificationException`.
- Remove allowed error from `GenerativeUnmappedLoadRestTest`

commit_message:
[TEST] Fix MLModelDeploymentFullClusterRestartIT {cluster=OLD} Failures

The {cluster=OLD} variant of testDeploymentSurvivesRestart has been failing at 100% on the 8.0.1 and 8.1.3 BWC steps despite two prior fix attempts (#145159, #145690). The root cause is that PyTorch model deployment inference on those very old cluster versions has never been reliable in CI — the feature was experimental in 8.0.x–8.2.x — and the test has been muted for this reason repeatedly since 2022. This PR adds an assumeTrue version guard to skip the old-cluster path when the old cluster is before 8.3.0 (where the unified _infer endpoint and stable parameter names were introduced), mutes the test while the fix propagates to CI, and removes the now-dead code: the oldClusterHasInferEndpoint() helper, the deployment/_infer fallback branch, the deprecated inference_threads/model_threads parameter branching in startDeployment, and the response format ternary in assertInfer.

Closes #146030.

issue_title: ES|QL: False-positive circular view reference when composing a subquery-based view with a simple view that shares a wildcard index pattern
commit_message:
Fix false circular view reference with shared wildcards (#146144)

Fixes #146097. When a subquery-based view (e.g., `FROM (FROM
app-events-* ...), (FROM auth-events-* ...)`) is composed with a simple
view that shares a wildcard index pattern (e.g., `FROM auth-events-*`),
querying the composed view incorrectly throws a "circular view
reference" error.                                                       


The root cause is that `Node.transformDown()` always recurses into the
children of a transformed node. After `replaceViewsUnresolvedRelation`
resolves views and returns a fully-resolved plan, `transformDown`
re-visits the children, causing wildcards inside resolved view
subqueries to be re-resolved. This produces:

1. **False circular reference errors** — sibling view names accumulate in the shared `seenViews` set, so a wildcard matching a sibling view's name appears circular
2. **Deeply nested duplicate plans** — re-resolution creates new `ViewUnionAll` nodes recursively up to the max view depth

The fix tracks resolved plan nodes in an identity set. When
`transformDown` later visits those nodes during its child recursion,
they are recognized as already-resolved and skipped. The unnecessary
`seenViews.add(view.name())` that polluted the shared seen set with
sibling view names is also removed.

In addition ViewUnionAll flattening has been enhanced to also flatten
included subqueries, so it becomes possible to have views that contain
subqueries without generating a `Nested subqueries are not supported`
error, as long as the subqueries inside the view are the top-most plan
node (ie. no following commands after the subqueries inside the view).

## Test plan

  - [x] New: `testFalseCircularReferenceFromSharedWildcardPattern_Issue146097` — exact reproduction from #146097
  - [x] New: `testFalseCircularReferenceWithSharedWildcardInSubqueries` — two views sharing a wildcard pattern composed in a parent view (view names don't match wildcards)
  - [x] New: `testFalseCircularReferenceWhenWildcardMatchesSiblingViewName` — wildcard in a subquery matches a sibling view's name (with self-exclusion)
  - [x] New: `testGenuineSelfReferenceViaWildcardInComposedView` — genuine circular self-reference via wildcard is still correctly detected
  - [x] Enhance view flattening phase to include directly nested subqueries and fork
  - [x] Update tests to verify that subquery flattening works
  - [x] All 101 `InMemoryViewServiceTests` pass                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  - [x] Verified new tests fail without the fix    
  - [x] Verify CsvIT tests still pass      

Assisted by Claude

Fixes #146097 Fixes #146118

commit_message:
Fix DataStreamIndexSettingsProviderTests setting count (#146395)

TIME_SERIES_USE_SYNTHETIC_ID_DEFAULT_PROD (9_093_0_00) and
TIME_SERIES_DISABLE_SEQUENCE_NUMBERS_DEFAULT (9_094_0_00) have different
version thresholds. maybeAdjustIndexSettingCount was incorrectly
assuming both settings are added together, causing off-by-one failures
when the random index version falls between the two thresholds.

Track expectedSyntheticId independently and increment the count
separately for each setting.

Closes #146126 Closes #146108 Closes #146109 Closes #146112 Closes
#146197 Closes #146198 Closes #146305 Closes #146307

issue_title: ES|QL Views false circular reference error on shared index pattern
issue_body:
Circular reference detection is conservative — if View A and View B both query the same underlying index pattern (e.g., svc-auth-*), composing them in a nested view triggers a "circular view reference" error even though there's no actual cycle.

Example:

```
PUT _query/view/error_triage
{
"query": """
FROM (
FROM svc-gateway-*
| WHERE http.response.status_code >= 500
| EVAL service = "gateway",
error_detail = CONCAT("HTTP ", http.response.status_code::string)
| KEEP @timestamp, service, error_detail, source.ip
),
(
FROM svc-payments-*
| WHERE transaction.status IN ("failed", "timeout")
| EVAL service = "payments",
error_detail = transaction.status
| KEEP @timestamp, service, error_detail, source.ip
),
(
FROM svc-auth-*
| WHERE event.action == "login" AND event.outcome == "failure"
| EVAL service = "auth",
error_detail = CONCAT(event.action, " ", event.outcome)
| KEEP @timestamp, service, error_detail, source.ip
)
"""
}
```

```
PUT _query/view/suspicious_ips
{
"query": """
FROM svc-auth-*
| WHERE event.action == "login" AND event.outcome == "failure"
| STATS attempts = COUNT(*),
first_seen = FIRST(@timestamp, @timestamp),
last_seen  = LAST(@timestamp, @timestamp)
BY source.ip
| WHERE attempts > 3
"""
}
```

then if you

```
PUT _query/view/incident_dashboard
{
"query": """
FROM error_triage, suspicious_ips
"""
}
```

and then query `FROM incident_dashboard`, you get an error

```
{
"error": {
"type": "verification_exception",
"reason": "circular view reference 'suspicious_ips': incident_dashboard -> error_triage -> suspicious_ips"
},
"status": 400
}
```
commit_message:
Fix false circular view reference with shared wildcards (#146144)

Fixes #146097. When a subquery-based view (e.g., `FROM (FROM
app-events-* ...), (FROM auth-events-* ...)`) is composed with a simple
view that shares a wildcard index pattern (e.g., `FROM auth-events-*`),
querying the composed view incorrectly throws a "circular view
reference" error.                                                       


The root cause is that `Node.transformDown()` always recurses into the
children of a transformed node. After `replaceViewsUnresolvedRelation`
resolves views and returns a fully-resolved plan, `transformDown`
re-visits the children, causing wildcards inside resolved view
subqueries to be re-resolved. This produces:

1. **False circular reference errors** — sibling view names accumulate in the shared `seenViews` set, so a wildcard matching a sibling view's name appears circular
2. **Deeply nested duplicate plans** — re-resolution creates new `ViewUnionAll` nodes recursively up to the max view depth

The fix tracks resolved plan nodes in an identity set. When
`transformDown` later visits those nodes during its child recursion,
they are recognized as already-resolved and skipped. The unnecessary
`seenViews.add(view.name())` that polluted the shared seen set with
sibling view names is also removed.

In addition ViewUnionAll flattening has been enhanced to also flatten
included subqueries, so it becomes possible to have views that contain
subqueries without generating a `Nested subqueries are not supported`
error, as long as the subqueries inside the view are the top-most plan
node (ie. no following commands after the subqueries inside the view).

## Test plan

  - [x] New: `testFalseCircularReferenceFromSharedWildcardPattern_Issue146097` — exact reproduction from #146097
  - [x] New: `testFalseCircularReferenceWithSharedWildcardInSubqueries` — two views sharing a wildcard pattern composed in a parent view (view names don't match wildcards)
  - [x] New: `testFalseCircularReferenceWhenWildcardMatchesSiblingViewName` — wildcard in a subquery matches a sibling view's name (with self-exclusion)
  - [x] New: `testGenuineSelfReferenceViaWildcardInComposedView` — genuine circular self-reference via wildcard is still correctly detected
  - [x] Enhance view flattening phase to include directly nested subqueries and fork
  - [x] Update tests to verify that subquery flattening works
  - [x] All 101 `InMemoryViewServiceTests` pass                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  - [x] Verified new tests fail without the fix    
  - [x] Verify CsvIT tests still pass      

Assisted by Claude
